vcenter.name: vCenter
steps: Upgrade vCenter to version 9.1 using VCF Operations through Fleet Management - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

esx.name: ESX
steps: All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Upgrade Virtual Distributed Switch - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
(Optional) Upgrade vSAN On-Disk Format Versions - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Check HCL for Hardware support on VCF 9.1
bullets: Intel Skylake (Broadwell, Kabylake & Skylake) Processors no longer supported (deprecated in earlier releases), requires RPQ

operations.name: VCF Operations (Aria Operations) Upgrade
steps: Upgrade VCF Operations to version 9.1 compatible release - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VCF Operations Information
bullets: Decommissioning of the 9.0 Fleet Management Appliance: VCF 9.1 completely replaces the standalone Fleet Management Appliance introduced in version 9.0. It is replaced with two new services—Fleet Lifecycle and SDDC Lifecycle—which run natively within VCF management services. The Fleet Management appliance is powered down and ready to be decommissioned.

sub_bullets: When attempting to upgrade to VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) 9.1, users may find that while the version 9.1 option is available in the SDDC Manager Binary Management dropdown menu, no binaries are displayed for any category (Upgrade, Patch, or Install) - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Upgrading VCF Operations from 9.0.2 to 9.1 returns sync error when upgrading ESXi host - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

steps: Deploy license server and registration - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

vcfms.name: VCF Management Service Deployment
steps: Deploy VCF management services for 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VCF Management Services
bullets: This path does NOT include VCF Automation deployment
Minimum 12 IP addresses required for deployment.

bullets.text: An additional 18 IP addresses can be added afterwards for the following scenarios:
sub_bullets: New VCF management services are added.
Scale out of VCF Management services or new nodes as upgrade to future versions take place.
A maximum of 30 IP address is required, these can be added as new IP ranges in the Lifecycle section of the VCF Operations UI, these ranges do not need to be continuous.
All IP Ranges must be on the VCF Management Network.

An internal IP range is used by VCF services runtime, this IP range is 198.18.0.0/15, it is important to ensure this IP range does not overlap with the IP range of your management network, this internal network can be changed to 240.0.0.0/15 or 250.0.0.0/15 by using JSON specification file to deploy.

vxrail.name: Dell VxRail
steps: Please Reach out to Dell on Steps and Services

notices.title: VxRail Upgrades
bullets: All upgrades for VxRail Appliances will be validated thru Dell support team or Dell Field/Partner(s).There are different pre-check and Solve Desktop Procedure based on the VxRail Hardware and VxRail versions. It is recommended not to attempt online upgrade thru VxRail Manager UI/VxL Manager vCenter Plugin.

vidb_overlay_transition.name: Transition Identity Broker to VCF Management Network
steps: Transition Identity Broker 9.0.x Instance to the VCF Management Network - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: This step must be completed first
bullets: If Identity Broker 9.0.x is on a non-mgmt network (regular vlan or NSX overlay) – please contact support for guidance and there will be downtime during the upgrade for VMware Identity Broker and Single Sign-On.
